title: Amplify Node-Type Templates
description: The system-owned registry of graph node types. Every node in a folded task graph (schemas/task-graph.schema.json) carries a required, explicit `type` that names one of these templates; scripts/task.mjs reads THIS file at load time and validates each node against its type's template. A template names: the properties that are required on a node of that type, an optional set of additional allowed properties, and an optional per-type executor rule. `deps` and `type` are required on every type. `executor` is required only on types that dispatch a subagent (implement, resolve, audit, reduce, agent); deterministic types (fn, expand, switch) have no executor rule and the engine drives them directly. Property semantics (id/deps/executor grammar, max_attempts integer, etc.) are the same as task-graph.schema.json -- this file only declares which properties belong to which type, plus the optional executor constraint.

implement.description: An implement-and-audit task authored by the execute-plan dump. Its implementer always runs subagent(general-purpose); specialized GUI/behavioral work is an audit node (authored executor), never an implementer.

resolve.description: The audit-resolver subnode for a completed task: it designs the auditor panel. Created at runtime.

audit.description: One auditor that verifies a task's acceptance criteria against the implementer's diff, blind and read-only. Its executor is an authored sub-agent chosen at runtime by the audit-resolver (any value matching the executor grammar).

reduce.description: Reduces one audit round to a verdict, keyed by a counter. Its executor names subagent(amplify:audit-reducer) (registry name only; the agent file does not exist yet).

agent.description: A non-deterministic node dispatched to a subagent. The subagent reads its inputs by reference from the value store (via resolve-context), runs the prompt, and writes a typed output back to the store. The orchestrator passes only handles in and reads only a handle/status out — the output bytes never enter its context. output_schema declares the type of the output value; it must be one of the minimal enumerable types (boolean, integer, string, array, object, optionally with enum).

fn.description: A deterministic node that runs a pure module export. The engine reads the node's input envelopes from the value store, calls the export with them, and writes the typed output to the store. A lone ready fn may run inline in commit; several ready fns run as parallel background exec-node tasks — both paths write only a handle, never the bytes, to the orchestrator. `require` controls scheduling: 'all-done' (default) waits for every dep to be done; 'all-resolved' (reducer/gather) runs when every dep is resolved (done or failed).

expand.description: Fan-out combinator: when its upstream node's output exists, the engine reads the `over` node's output (an array), creates one child from `template` per element in a single commit, and wires each child to `gather`. Each element is bound by reference (a per-element store entry), so the orchestrator dispatches children with handles only. An empty list creates no children.

switch.description: Branch combinator: when its selector node's output exists, the engine instantiates only the matching case in a single commit. `over` is the id of the selector node; `cases` is an object whose keys are the case values (strings: 'true'/'false' for boolean; enum values for an enum selector). validateGraph statically enforces exhaustiveness: the case set must exactly equal the selector's declared domain (boolean -> {true,false}; enum -> every enum value). A non-enumerable selector (type is not boolean and no enum) is rejected at init time.
